calendar.matters:
Petition For Acceptance Of Resignation Of Trustee And For Appointment Of Successor Trustees
Off Calendar Jun-25-2020 Off Calendar

Petition For Acceptance Of Resignation Of Trustee And For Appointment Of Successor Trustees
Off Calendar Mar-25-2020 Continued To Sep-22-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Acceptance Of Resignation Of Trustee And For Appointment Of Successor Trustees
Off Calendar Mar-03-2020 Continued To Jun-30-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Acceptance Of Resignation Of Trustee And For Appointment Of Successor Trustees
Off Calendar Dec-17-2019 Continued To Mar-03-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Acceptance Of Resignation Of Trustee And For Appointment Of Successor Trustees
Off Calendar Oct-15-2019 Continued To Dec-17-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Edward Koplowitz for Mark Hancock.
Nancy Rasch for and with Debra Dolch (Trustee).
David Zeligs appeared by telephone for and with Marian Hancock (present in Court).
Marsha Adams appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

Mr. Koplowitz stated that the three Beneficiaries are requesting a six week continuance, that the accounting is two years out of date and needs to be brought to date to allow the parties to look at it, that some of the assets are oil and gas leases which are unusual and difficult, and that it is going to be a daunting task. Ms. Rasch stated that they will file an accounting, that she hoped a family member would come in so that Ms. Dolch could do a final accounting and that she is happy that there is some movement. Mr. Zeligs asked for clarification as to whether Mark provided a W-9, raised the issue of income coming from assets that are not titled in the trust and the Trustee receiving fees, and stated that he feels more time is needed to get assets distributed. Ms. Rasch stated that mark provided a W-9 on October 11th, that they can proceed on distributing assets, that she is reluctant to have a longer continuance because there is no movement when there is no deadline and that the Trustee's fees are not before the Court. The Petition for Acceptance of Resignation of Trustee and Appointment of Successor Trustees is continued to December 17, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-18-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nancy Rasch for and with Debra Dolch (Trustee).
Edward Koplowitz for and with Mark Hancock (Beneficiary/Objector).
Marian Hancock (Beneficiary) in Propria Persona.

Ms. Rasch stated that the objections were on the grounds that the Trustee's fees at the 1% valuation of the assets was incorrect, so they asked for the hourly rate, and they are now objecting to that. Ms. Rasch also stated that they are claiming there are additional assets (oil/gas leases) that were never collected by the Trustee, that the documents are from 1960, that there was a divorce, that this is a twenty year old probate and that she does not believe they are assets of the trust. Ms. Rasch also stated that some years ago the Beneficiaries were told they needed to open probate, that these are primarily assets of the estate, and that she does not know if they are part of the Trust or viable assets. Mr. Koplowitz stated that they should have corrected the valuation, that $14,000.00 was based on valuation of ten years ago, that there is about $100,000.00 in cash in the estate, that the Trustee is asking for a reserve of $30,000.00, that there have been no preliminary distributions to any of the Beneficiaries and that his Client has spent hours on these assets. Mr. Koplowitz also spoke of the duty of the Trustee to locate, marshal assets and bring them into the Trust, and stated that they can do the leg work and set a budget of $100,000.00. Ms. Rasch stated that this accounting has been continued several times, that she never got a letter from Mr. Koplowitz, that she finally received two hundred pages of documents and that no one ever raised any issues until years after the Settlor's death. The Court stated that if all of the Beneficiaries are on board, they can work it out, present a budget to the Trustee and if the Trustee does not want to do it, the Executor can open probate. The Petition for Final Distribution of Trust, etc. is under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Off Calendar May-16-2018 Continued To Aug-15-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ed Koplowtiz for Mark Hancock (Beneficiary)
Nancy Rasch for and with Debra Dolch
Maryann Hancock in propria persona

Ms. Rasch stated this matter was continued a number of times at the request of family members and Mr. Koplowitz, and that she will need time to bring the accounting forward. Ms. Rasch requested a continuance to August to address the Probate Examiner's notes and give notice for an amended accounting petition. Mr. Koplowitz stated that he filed something on Monday, a 3rd beneficiary/child may have sent a letter to the court, and would like to report back to the court at the next hearing date. The Petition for Final Distribution of Trust, etc. is continued to August 15, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Ms. Rasch to respond to Probate Examiner's notes and amend the petition to bring the accounting forward.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Jan-13-2010 Continued To Feb-24-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 282471 Conservatorship of Westley Hancock.

Appearances: Janet Boessenecker for Linda Harrington (Conservator/Trustee).

Ms. Boessenecker stated that she received a draft of the accounting a couple of weeks ago, that she returned it to Ms. Harrington with some suggestions, that she did not get a hard copy back from Ms. Harrington and that she had not heard from her. An Order to Show Cause will be issued to Linda Harrington on the Conservatorship matter and the Trust matter to appear and show cause why she should not be punished for contempt for failure to file the Fourth Account on both matters. The return date on both matters is February 24, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Sanctions in the amount of $25.00 per day on each matter will be imposed against Linda Harrington and will start on that date if the accounting is not filed. Status of filing of the 4th Account on both matters were continued to February 24, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.
